examples.title: Algorithm: Concatenate Two Linked Lists
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating the concatenation of two linked lists
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ConcatenateLists
INPUT: head1, head2 (pointers to first node of each list)
OUTPUT: pointer to concatenated list

BEGIN
 IF head1 is NULL THEN
 RETURN head2
 END IF

 current = head1
 WHILE current->next is not NULL DO
 current = current->next
 END WHILE

 current->next = head2
 RETURN head1
END

examples.title: Python: Concatenate Two Linked Lists
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating the concatenation of two linked lists
examples.code:
class Node:
 def __init__(self, data):
 self.data = data
 self.next = None

class LinkedList:
 def __init__(self):
 self.head = None

 def concatenate(self, list2):
 if self.head is None:
 return list2
 current = self.head
 while current.next is not None:
 current = current.next
 current.next = list2.head
 return self.head

# Example usage:
list1 = LinkedList()
list1.head = Node(1)
list1.head.next = Node(2)
list1.head.next.next = Node(3)

list2 = LinkedList()
list2.head = Node(4)
list2.head.next = Node(5)
list2.head.next.next = Node(6)

result = list1.concatenate(list2)
while result is not None:
 print(result.data, end=' ')
 result = result.next
examples.explanation: Key implementation details and how it relates to the concept
